name: Deriv Volatility 100 (1s variant exists as 1HZ100V)
trading_hours: 24/7/365 - never closes, no sessions, no news, no gaps

engine.how_it_moves: RNG-generated price calibrated to a constant 100% annualized volatility - the fastest of your three synthetics. Tick every ~2 seconds. Sessions, time of day, and news are all irrelevant.
engine.critical_implication: Same technicals as V75 but every move, wick, and stop-out is bigger. Position size must be the smallest of all your instruments.

personality.summary: The high-octane synthetic. Huge candles, brutal spike wicks, fast trend legs and equally fast reversals. Delivers the biggest R-multiples in the shortest time but punishes tight stops and oversized positions harder than anything else you trade. Treat it as V75's aggressive sibling: same playbook, wider stops, smaller size.
personality.typical_spread: Widest of the three synthetics - scalp targets must clear the spread comfortably
behavioral_patterns: Trend legs travel further and faster than V75; pullbacks are deeper (50-61.8% is normal)
Spike wicks of 3x ATR against the trend are routine - then price resumes
Ranges are violent: both edges get swept before the true break
M1 is nearly pure noise - execute on M5 minimum

key_technicals.moving_averages: M15 21 EMA + H1 50 EMA trend stack; only trade when both agree
key_technicals.structure: BOS/CHoCH on M15 is the primary signal; M5 structure alone whipsaws too much
key_technicals.atr: Stops = 2.5-3x ATR(M5) minimum. If that stop makes your size feel tiny, that is correct
key_technicals.fibonacci: Deep 61.8-78.6% retracements of impulse legs are the premium continuation entries
key_technicals.rsi: M15 RSI divergence at range extremes is reliable; ignore all M1 RSI signals

scalping_plan.sessions: Any hour 24/7 - but V100 scalps are really 'fast day trades': M5-based, wider targets

steps: Wait for M15 pullback into the 50-78.6% fib zone of the last impulse that overlaps an order block
M5 trigger: engulfing or double-bottom/top at the zone
SL 2.5x ATR(M5) beyond the zone, TP 1:1.5 fixed - do not get greedy on scalps here
Identify M15 range with at least 2 touches per side
Wait for BOTH edges to be swept (V100 signature), then trade the third move from the reclaimed edge
SL beyond the latest sweep, TP opposite edge

scalping_plan.risk: Max 0.5% risk per trade on V100 - half your normal risk. One oversized V100 loss undoes a week of scalps. Hard rule: 2 consecutive V100 losses = switch to V50/V75 for the rest of the session.

day_trading_plan.model: H1 impulse rider - highest R-multiple instrument you trade
steps: Enter on M15 CHoCH at an H1 zone after a deep pullback
Partial at 1R immediately (spike insurance), trail rest behind M15 swings
Trend legs of 4-8R are realistic - let the runner run until H1 structure breaks

after_hours_00_05_sast.conditions: Identical to every other hour - constant engineered volatility.
after_hours_00_05_sast.verdict: FULLY TRADEABLE, but the combination of maximum instrument volatility + trader fatigue at night is dangerous. If you trade V100 in this window, cut size a further 50%.
playbook: Prefer setup_1 (with-trend) only; skip range trades at night
Cap the window at 3 V100 trades
If you feel the urge to revenge trade after a V100 stop-out, close the platform - this instrument exploits tilt

invalidations_and_traps: Tight stops are donations on V100 - if you cannot afford the 2.5-3x ATR stop, skip the trade
Chasing a running candle is fatal: entries are at zones only, never mid-impulse
Martingale/grid recovery on V100 destroys accounts faster than on any other instrument
M1 signals are noise - if your trigger only exists on M1, there is no trade
